title: Devices and methods for personalized percutaneous spine surgery
abstract: Provided herein are implant devices suitable for implantation, e.g., into bone, with a body of a patient. The implant devices have proximal, distal, and middle sections, wherein the middle section has a stiffness different from, e.g., greater than, that of the proximal and distal sections. Also provided are methods for implanting the provided devices.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An elongated implant device for implantation into bone within a patient body, the implant device comprising: a proximal section comprising a proximal section outer surface; a distal section comprising a distal section outer surface; and a middle section having a middle section outer surface, wherein the middle section is located between and adjacent to the proximal and distal sections, wherein the middle section has a stiffness more than 10% different from those of the proximal and distal sections, and wherein the stiffness is selected from the group consisting of bending stiffness, torsional stiffness, axial stiffness, and combinations thereof. 2. The implant device of claim 1, wherein the middle section has a stiffness greater than those of the proximal and distal sections. 3. The implant device of claim 2, wherein the proximal and distal sections each independently have a bending stiffness less than 3.5 N m 2 , and wherein the middle section has a bending stiffness greater than 0.07 N m 2 . 4. The implant device of claim 3, wherein the proximal and distal sections each independently have a bending stiffness less than 3.5 N m 2 , and wherein the middle section has a bending stiffness greater than 0.3 N m 2 . 5. The implant device of claim 3, wherein the proximal and distal sections each independently have a bending stiffness less than 2.5 N m 2 , and wherein the middle section has a bending stiffness greater than 0.2 N m 2 . 6. The implant device of claim 3, wherein the proximal and distal sections each independently have a bending stiffness less than 0.8 N m 2 , and wherein the middle section has a bending stiffness greater than 0.07 N m 2 . 7. The implant device of any one of claims 2-6, wherein the ratios of the middle section bending stiffness to the proximal section bending stiffness and the distal section bending stiffness are each independently between 1.1 : 1 and 500: 1. 8 The implant device of any one of claims 2-7, further comprising: 

 a compliant member located within one or more of the proximal, distal, and middle sections; and a stiffening member located within one or more of the proximal, distal, and middle sections, wherein the stiffening member has a stiffness greater than that of the compliant member, and wherein the volume percentage of the middle section comprising the stiffening member is greater than the volume percentage of the proximal section comprising the stiffening member, and the volume percentage of the distal section comprising the stiffening member. 9. The implant device of claim 8, wherein the moment of inertia of a cross section of the stiffening member in the middle section is greater than the moment of inertia of a cross section of the stiffening member in the proximal section when present, and the moment of inertia of a cross section of the stiffening member of the distal section when present. 10. The implant device of claim 8 or 9, wherein the polar moment of inertia of a cross section of the stiffening member in the middle section is greater than the polar moment of inertia of a cross section of the stiffening member in the proximal section when present, and the polar moment of inertia of a cross section of the stiffening member of the distal section when present. 11. The implant device of any one of claims 8-10, wherein at least a portion of the compliant member forms a mantle surrounding a core formed from at least a portion of the stiffening member. 12. The implant device of any one of claims 8-10, wherein at least a portion of the stiffening member forms a mantle surrounding a core formed from at least a portion of the compliant member. 13. The implant device of claim 11 or 12, wherein the core comprises an outer surface having a non-uniform diameter, and wherein the mantle comprises an inner surface having a shape complementary to that of the core outer surface. 14. The implant device of any one of claims 8-13, wherein the compliant member comprises one or more polymers or polymer composites, and wherein the stiffening member comprises one or more metals, ceramics, or a combination thereof. 15. The implant device of any one of claims 2-7, further comprising: one or more slots on at least a portion of one or both of the distal section outer surface and the proximal section outer surface. 16. The implant device of claim 15, wherein the one or more slots are sufficient to reduce the stiffness of the section(s) comprising the one or more slots by at least 10% as compared to the stiffness of the corresponding section(s) not comprising the one or more slots. 17. The implant device of claim 15 or 16, wherein each of the one or more slots independently has a depth at least 5% of the diameter of the section comprising the slot. 18. The implant device of any one of claims 15-17, wherein each of the one or more slots is configured in a helical shape. 19. The implant device of any one of claims 15-17, wherein each of the slots is configured in a circumferential shape. 20. The implant device of any one of claims 15-17, wherein each of the one or more slots is independently parallel to the diameter of the section comprising the slot, and perpendicular to the axis of the implant device. 21. The implant device of claim 20, comprising two or more slots, wherein each of the two or more slots has an alignment angulated or orthogonal to that of an adjacent slot. 22. The implant device of any one of claims 2-7, further comprising: one or more helical coils on or near at least a portion of one or both of the distal section outer surface and the proximal section outer surface. 23. The implant device of any one of claims 2-7, further comprising: a counter-wound coil having two or more coil layers on or near at least a portion of the middle section outer surface. 24. The implant device of claim 1, wherein the middle section has a stiffness less than those of the proximal and distal sections. 25. The implant device of claim 24, wherein the proximal and distal sections each independently have a bending stiffness greater than 0.07 N m 2 , and wherein the middle section has a bending stiffness less than 3.5 N m 2 . 26. The implant device of claim 25, wherein the proximal and distal sections each independently have a bending stiffness greater than 0.3 N m 2 , and wherein the middle section has a bending stiffness less than 3.5 N m 2 . 27. The implant device of claim 25, wherein the proximal and distal sections each independently have a bending stiffness greater than 0.2 N m 2 , and wherein the middle section has a bending stiffness less than 2.5 N m 2 . 28. The implant device of claim 25, wherein the proximal and distal sections each independently have a bending stiffness greater than 0.07 N m 2 , and wherein the middle section has a bending stiffness less than 0.8 N m 2 . 29. The implant device of any one of claims 24-28, wherein the ratios of the proximal section bending stiffness and the distal section bending stiffness to the middle section bending stiffness are each independently between 1.1 : 1 and 500: 1. 30. The implant device of any one of claims 24-29, further comprising: a compliant member located within one or more of the proximal, distal, and middle sections; and a stiffening member located within one or more the proximal, distal, and middle sections, wherein the stiffening member has a stiffness higher than that of the compliant member, and wherein the volume percentage of the middle section comprising the stiffening member is less than the volume percentage of the proximal section comprising the stiffening member, and the volume percentage of the distal section comprising the stiffening member. 31. The implant device of claim 30, wherein the moment of inertia of a cross section of the stiffening member in the middle section is less than the moment of inertia of a cross section of the stiffening member in the proximal section when present, and the moment of inertia of a cross section of the stiffening member of the distal section when present. 32. The implant device of claim 30 or 31, wherein the polar moment of inertia of a cross section of the stiffening member in the middle section is greater than the polar moment of inertia of a cross section of the stiffening member in the proximal section when present, and the polar moment of inertia of a cross section of the stiffening member of the distal section when present. 33. The implant device of any one of claims 30-32, wherein at least a portion of the compliant member forms a mantle surrounding a core formed from at least a portion of the stiffening member. 34. The implant device of any one of claims 30-32, wherein at least a portion of the stiffening member forms a mantle surrounding a core formed from at least a portion of the compliant member. 35. The implant device of claim 33 or 34, wherein the core comprises an outer surface having a non-uniform diameter, and wherein the mantle comprises an inner surface having a shape complementary to that of the core outer surface. 36. The implant device of any one of claims 30-35, wherein the compliant member comprises one or more polymers or polymer composites, and wherein the stiffening member comprises one or more metals, ceramics, or a combination thereof. 37. The implant device of any one of claims 24-29, further comprising: one or more slots on at least a portion of the middle section outer surface. 38. The implant device of claim 37, wherein the one or more slots are sufficient to reduce the stiffness of the middle section by at least 10% as compared to the stiffness of the corresponding middle section not comprising the one or more slots. 39. The implant device of claim 37 or 38, wherein each of the one or more slots independently has a depth at least 5% of the diameter of the section comprising the slot. 40. The implant device of any one of claims 37-39, wherein each of the one or more slots is configured in a helical shape. 41. The implant device of any one of claims 37-39, wherein each of the slots is configured in a circumferential shape. 42. The implant device of any one of claims 37-39, wherein each of the one or more slots is independently parallel to the diameter of the section comprising the slot, and perpendicular to the axis of the implant device. 43. The implant device of claim 42, comprising two or more slots, wherein each of the two or more slots has an alignment angulated or orthogonal to that of an adjacent slot. 44. The implant device of any one of claims 24-29, further comprising: one or more helical coils on at least a portion of the middle section outer surface. 45. The implant device of any one of claims 24-29, further comprising: two or more counter-wound coils on at least a portion of one or both of the distal section outer surface and the proximal section outer surface. 46. The implant device of any one of claims 1-45, further comprising: distal threading on at least a portion of the distal section outer surface. 47. The implant device of any one of claims 1-46, further comprising: proximal threading on at least a portion of the proximal section outer surface. 48. The implant device of claim 47, comprising distal threading on at least a portion of the distal section outer surface, wherein the distal threading has a pitch larger than that of the proximal threading, and wherein the distal threading has a smaller diameter than that of the proximal threading. 49. The implant device of any one of claims 1-48, wherein the middle section comprises a spring connected to the proximal and distal sections, and wherein the spring is configured to be in a less relaxed position as the distance between the proximal and distal sections is increased. 50. The implant device of claim 49, wherein the spring is connected to a spring screw configured to extend the spring upon rotation of the spring screw. 51. The implant device of any one of claims 1-49, further comprising: 

 a lumen passing through each of the proximal, middle, and distal sections, wherein the proximal section comprises a proximal opening of the lumen, and wherein the distal end comprises a distal opening of the lumen. 52. The implant device of any one of claims 1-51, further comprising: a head adjacent to the proximal section opposite the middle section. 53. The implant device of any one of claims 1-52, further comprising: a coating comprising a bone growth stimulant. 54. The implant device of claim 53, wherein the bone growth stimulant comprises hydroxyapatite. 55. A method for implanting an implant device into a bone within a patient body, the method comprising: providing the implant device of any one of claims 1-54; drilling a hole into the bone within the patient body, thereby removing a portion of the bone to form the hole; and inserting the implant device into the hole such that the distal section of the implant device engages with the bone defining the hole. 56. The method of claim 55, wherein the distal section of the implant device has a stiffness that is within 40% of that of the removed portion of the bone. 57. The method of claim 55 or 56, further comprising: obtaining from preoperative or intraoperative images an insertion location of the patient body, a delivery depth within the patient body, and an insertion trajectory angle or a projection thereof in the plane of the images; obtaining or calculating a first angle of advancement in a first plane parallel to the skin surface of the patient; obtaining or calculating a second angle of advancement in a second plane orthogonal to the first plane; positioning the implant device at the insertion location; angulating the implant device under the first and second angle of advancement; and advancing the implant device within the patient body to the delivery depth. 58. The method of claim 55 or 56, further comprising: obtaining from preoperative or intraoperative imaging the coordinates of an insertion location of the patient body, and the coordinates of a delivery location within the patient body; obtaining from preoperative or intraoperative imaging the coordinates of three nonlinear calibration points external to the patient body or on a skin surface of the patient; calibrating a coordinate system of a delivery tool using the coordinates of the three nonlinear calibration points; positioning, using the delivery tool, the implant device at the insertion location; angulating, using the delivery tool, the implant device at the insertion location to align with a vector joining the coordinates of the insertion location and the coordinates of the delivery location; and advancing the implant device to the delivery location. 59. The method of claim 58, wherein the delivery tool comprises a robot. 60. An adhesive plaster for application on the skin of a patient, the plaster comprising: one or more lines of contrast markers visible under X-ray imaging, MRI imaging, or a combination thereof; and one or more holes or slots configured to permit a marking tip of a pen to pass therethrough. 61. An adhesive plaster for application on the skin of a patient, the plaster comprising: one or more lines of contrast markers visible under X-ray imaging, MRI imaging, or a combination thereof; and one or more regions of transferrable ink corresponding to the shapes and locations of the one or more lines of contrast markers, wherein the regions are configured to transfer at least a portion of the transferrable ink to the skin of the patient when the plater is applied to the skin. 62. An adhesive plaster for application on the skin of a patient, the plaster comprising: 

 one or more lines of contrast markers visible under X-ray imaging, MRI imaging, or a combination thereof, wherein the one or more lines are embedded in a top layer; and a bottom layer removably attached to the top layer, wherein the bottom layer comprises one or more holes or slots arranged in a pattern corresponding to the shapes and locations of the one or more lines of contrast markers, and wherein the holes or slots are configured to permit a marking tip of a pen to pass therethrough.
